capacity_evidence.probe_command: TT_LOGGER_LEVEL=fatal MISTRAL_SMALL_24B_CONTEXT_PROBE_LEN=<length> TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3 pytest -q -s models/autoports/mistralai_mistral_small_24b_instruct_2501/tests/test_context_capacity.py (add MISTRAL_SMALL_24B_CONTEXT_EXPECT_OOM=1 for the first failing length)
capacity_evidence.device: Blackhole p300c UMD, one logical 1x1 mesh device
capacity_evidence.probe_process_policy: one length per fresh process on a one-device mesh; full-shape synthetic BF16 weights have the same device allocation sizes as real weights; output is copied to host to force completion

first_failure.operation_class: device DRAM tensor allocation during prefill

capacity_evidence.post_failure_health_check: tt-smi -ls --local returned all four Blackhole chips after normal fixture teardown
capacity_evidence.explanation: At emitted batch 32, the current dense BF16 path completes at logical sequence length 3583 and the immediately adjacent length 3584 reproducibly fails a physical DRAM allocation, establishing the exact current boundary. HF context 32768 is also impossible for this path: one [32,32768,32768] BF16 MLP activation alone is 68,719,476,736 bytes, exceeding the measured 34,178,731,008 device bytes before weights, input, KV cache, or other live tensors.

ir_provenance.collective_policy: TP shards and all-reduces collapsed to equivalent dense single-device math over full unsharded Hugging Face weights

notes: This is the functional-decoder contract only. It does not claim optimized-decoder, multichip, full-model, or serving readiness.
